text: e, that for us. But here, there will be an imbalance in the microbiota that will be a bit disturbed, which will induce a somewhat inappropriate immune reaction. So all of that will create a slightly increased intestinal permeability, which means immune cells will come — they will be recruited to a place where they shouldn't be. And so they attack small pieces of the digestive tract, as I told you, in a segmental and focal way. And so it is a disease that progresses by flares interspersed with remission, which means that at some times things will be very bad, and then there will be times when it is calmer.
